storylets.title: {=floor_dispatch_march_at_dawn_title}March at dawn
storylets.setup: {=floor_dispatch_march_at_dawn_setup}Orders run down the line: march at dawn. The sergeants repeat it twice so no one mishears. Tents come down before the fires are out, and the horses go uneasy.

storylets.title: {=floor_dispatch_strike_camp_title}Strike camp
storylets.setup: {=floor_dispatch_strike_camp_setup}Strike camp. New heading, no time for questions. A courier came in at full gallop and left again before his horse had finished heaving. The sergeants are not telling the rest.

storylets.title: {=floor_dispatch_siege_lifted_title}Siege lifted
storylets.setup: {=floor_dispatch_siege_lifted_setup}The siege is lifted. The column moves out with the earthworks still warm. No one tells you whether it was a win or a retreat, and the older men do not ask.

options.tooltip: {=floor_dispatch_siege_lifted_tt}Take your place in the column. No effect.

storylets.title: {=floor_dispatch_army_breaking_title}The army disperses
storylets.setup: {=floor_dispatch_army_breaking_setup}Orders reach camp: the army disperses. Each lord to his own holdings, each banner to its own road. The courier does not stay to explain, and nobody chases him for it.

storylets.title: {=floor_dispatch_relief_column_title}A relief column
storylets.setup: {=floor_dispatch_relief_column_setup}A relief column is called for and we ride to join it. Someone somewhere is in worse shape than we are, which is a thought that keeps the men quiet in the saddle.
